assumption_text: The civilian exposure lower bound is the Tekuma resident population plus the conservative minimum Nova transient crowd; the upper bound is the Bank of Israel southern-sphere population of 57 western-Negev localities.
rationale: A full attacked-locality resident roll for every kibbutz, moshav, town, roadside cluster, and transient civilian site was not recovered in this pass.

assumption_text: Soldier exposure is modeled as 767 on 7 October plus bounded attacked-footprint reinforcement averages of 1,500 (best) or 3,000 (high) on 8-10 October.
rationale: The initial 767 count is explicit, but no public day-by-day soldier-only theater roster was recovered for the retake period.

assumption_text: Police, Shin Bet, and unresolved local-security officers are not silently merged into the soldier-only DR.
rationale: Their direct-death counts are partially recoverable, but their matched theater denominator is not.

assumption_text: Person-months are calculated over a 4-day episode and divided by 30.
rationale: The IDF probe framing and immediate retake narrative support a 7-10 October bounded window.

assumption_text: A subordinate Episode B scenario can be represented inside the combined Oct. 7 episode by restricting scope to attacked towns, kibbutzim, roads, and the Nova/Reim festival area while excluding a matched border-base Episode A split.
rationale: The workspace contains a usable Episode B locality reconstruction but no parallel Episode A file. Keeping the split as labeled scenario metrics is safer than pretending the current episode has been cleanly partitioned.
